sections.label: Section 300
sections.original:
305
κἄπειτ ’ ἐπᾴξας αὖθις ἐς δόμους πάλιν ,
sections.translation: he abused in their bonds as though they were men , though falling only upon cattle . At last he darted out through the door , and dragged up words to speak to some shadow — now against the Atreidae , now about Odysseus — with many a mocking boast of all the abuse that in vengeance he had fully repaid them during his raid .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original:
310
κόμην ἀπρὶξ ὄνυξι συλλαβὼν χερί .
sections.translation: After that he rushed back again into the house , and somehow by slow , painful steps he regained his reason . And as he scanned the room full of his disastrous madness , he struck his head and howled ; he fell down , a wreck amid the wrecked corpses of the slaughtered sheep , and there he sat

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original:
315
κἀγὼ , φίλοι , δείσασα τοὐ ἑιργασμένον
sections.translation: with clenched nails tightly clutching his hair . At first , and for a long while , he sat without a sound . But then he threatened me with those dreadful threats , if I did not declare all that had happened , and he demanded to know what on earth was the business he found himself in .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original:
320
τοιούςδ ’ ἀεί ποτ ’ ἀνδρὸς ὲξηγεῖτ ’ ἔχειν .
sections.translation: And in my fear , friends , I told him all that had been done , as far as I knew it for certain . But he immediately groaned mournful groans , such as I had never heard from him before . For he had always taught that such wailing

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original:
325
σιδηροκμῆσιν ἥσυχος θακεῖ πεσών .
sections.translation:
was for cowardly and low-hearted men . He used to grieve quietly without the sound of loud weeping , but instead moaned low like a bull .
And now , prostrate in such miserable fortune , tasting no food , no drink ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: 330 φίλων γὰρ οἱ τοιοίδε νικῶνται λόγοις .
sections.translation: the man sits idly where he has fallen in the middle of the iron-slain cattle . And plainly he plans to do something terrible . Somehow his words and his laments say as much . Ah , my friends — for it was my errand to ask you this — come in and help him , if in any way you can .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: Αἴαντος , οἵαν τήνδε θωύσσει βοήν ; 335
sections.translation: Men of his kind can be won over by the words of friends .

sections.label: Section 331
sections.original: Ἰώ μοί μοι .
sections.translation: Tecmessa , daughter of Teleutas , terrible is your news that our lord has been possessed by his sorrows .

sections.label: Section 333
sections.original: νοσήμασι ξυνοῦσι λυπεῖσθαι παρών .
sections.translation: Oh ! Ah , me ! Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: Ἰὼ παῖ , παῖ .
sections.translation: Soon there will be more sorrow , it seems . Did you not hear

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: Ὤ μοι τάλαιν ’, Εὐρύσακες , ἀμφὶ σοὶ βοᾷ . 340
sections.translation: Ajax , did you not hear his resounding howl ?

sections.label: Section 336
sections.original: τί ποτε μενοινᾷ ; ποῦ ποτ ’ εἶ ; τάλαιν ’ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Oh ! Ah , me ! Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 337
sections.original: Τεῦκρον καλῶ , ποῦ Τεῦκρος ; ἦ τὸν εἰςαεὶ
sections.translation: The man seems to be sick , or else to be still pained by the disease which was recently with him , since he sees its traces .

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: ἀνὴρ φρονεῖν ἔοικεν · ἀλλ ’ ἀνοίγετε .
sections.translation: Oh my son , my son !

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: τάχ ’ ἄν τιν ’ αἰδῶ κἀπ ’ ἐμοὶ βλέψας λάβοι . 345
sections.translation: Ah , miserable me ! Eurysaces , it is for you he calls ! What can his purpose be ? Where are you ? I am miserable !

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: τὰ τοῦδε πράγη , καὐτὸς ὡς ἔχων κυρεῖ .
sections.translation: Teucer ! Where is Teucer ? Will his raid last for ever ? While I perish ?

sections.label: Section 344
sections.original: φίλοι ναυβάται , μόνοι ἐμῶν φίλων
sections.translation: He seems to be sane — open the door !

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: μόνοι τ ’ ἐμμένοντες ὀρθῷ νόμῳ , 350
sections.translation: Perhaps merely at the sight of me he will come to a more respectable mood .

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: ἴδεσθέ μ ’ οἶον ἄρτι κῦμα φοινίας ὑπὸ ζάλης
sections.translation: There , it is open . Now you can look on this man ’ s deeds , and his true condition .

sections.label: Section 348
sections.original: οἴ μ ’, ὡς ἔοικας ὀρθὰ μαρτυρεῖν ἄγαν .
sections.translation: Ah , good sailors , you alone of my friends

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ἰὼ
sections.translation: who alone still abide by the true bond of friendship , see how great a wave has just now crested over and broken around me , set on by a murderous storm !

sections.label: Section 354
sections.original: ἀλλά με συνδάϊξον .
sections.translation: Ah , lady , too true , it seems , was your testimony !

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: εὔφημα φώνει · μὴ κακὸν κακῷ διδοὺς
sections.translation: The fact proves that he is not sane .

sections.label: Section 356
sections.original: ἄκος , πλέον τὸ πῆμα τῆς ἄτης τίθει .
sections.translation: Ah , you clan staunch in maritime skill , who embarked and stroked the oar-blade upon the brine , in you , in you alone

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: οἴ μοι γέλωτος , οἶον ὑβρίσθην ἄρα .
sections.translation: I see a defense against suffering . Come , slay me on top of these !

sections.label: Section 362
sections.original: Οὐκ ἐκτός ; οὐκ ἄψορρον ὲκνεμεῖ πόδα ;
sections.translation: Hush ! Speak words of better omen ! Do not cure evil by prescribing evil ; do not increase the anguish of your mad disaster .

sections.label: Section 364
sections.original: ὦ πρὸς θεῶν , ὕπεικε καὶ φρόνησον εὖ .
sections.translation: Do you see the bold , the strong of heart ,

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ὦ , δύςμορος , ὅς χερὶ μὲν
sections.translation: the dauntless in battles with the enemy — do you see me now , terrible in the force of my hands against beasts unformidable ? Oh , the mockery ! How I have been violated !

sections.label: Section 368
sections.original:
375
κλυτοῖς πεσὼν αἰπολίοις ,
sections.translation: Ajax , my lord , I beg you , do not talk this way !

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: ἐρεμνὸν αἷμ ’ ἔδευσα .
sections.translation: Away with you ! Will you not take yourself outside !

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: Τί δῆτ ’ ἂν ἀλγοίης ἐπ ’ ἐξειργασμένοις ·
sections.translation: Ah , me ! Ah , me !

sections.label: Section 371
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ γένοιτ ’ ἂν ταῦθ ’ ὅπως οὐχ ὧδ ’ ἔχειν .
sections.translation: By the gods I plead , give way and acquire good sense !

sections.label: Section 372
sections.original: Ἰὼ πάνθ ’ ὁρῶν , ἀπάντων ἀεὶ
sections.translation: My rotten fortune !— I let slip from my grasp those men who were begging for punishment ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ἦ που πολὺν γέλωθ ’ ὑφ ’ ἡδονἠς ἄγεις .
sections.translation: and fell instead on cattle with twisted horns and fine goats , making their dark blood flow !

sections.label: Section 377
sections.original: Ἴδοιμι δή νιν , καίπερ ὧδ ’ ἀτώμενος .
sections.translation: Why grieve when the deed is past recall ? These things can never be but as they are .

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: Μηδὲν μέγ ’ εἴπῃς · οὐχ ὁρᾷς , ἵν ’ εἶ κακοῦ ;
sections.translation: Ah , you who spy out all things ,

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , προγόνων προπάτωρ ,
sections.translation: you ready tool of every crime , ah , son of Laertes , you filthiest sneak in all the army , I am sure you laugh loud and long for joy !

sections.label: Section 383
sections.original:
390
σάρχας ὀλέσσας βασιλῆς ,
sections.translation: It is at the god ’ s dispensation that every man both laughs and mourns .

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: τέλος θάνοιμι καὐτός !
sections.translation: Yet if only I could see him , even shattered as I am !

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ὅταν κατεύχῃ ταῦθ ’, ὁμοῦ κἀμοὶ θανεῖν
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh !

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: εὔχου · τί γὰρ δεῖ ζῆν με , σοῦ τεθνηκότος ;
sections.translation: Make no big threats ! Do you not see the trouble you are in ?

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: Ἰὼ σκότος , ἐμὸν φάος ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , forefather of my forebears , if only I might destroy that deep dissembler , that hateful sneak , and

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἕλεσθέ μ ’, οὔτε γὰρ θεῶν
sections.translation: the two brother-kings , and finally die myself , also !

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: ἔτ ’ ἄξιος βλέπειν τίν ’
sections.translation: When you make that prayer , pray at the same time for me that I , too , may die . What reason is there for me to live when you are dead ?

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: ἀλλά μ ’ ἁ Δῖός
sections.translation: Ah , Darkness , my light !

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ἀλκίμα θεὸς
sections.translation: O Gloom of the underworld , to my eyes brightest-shining , take me , take me to dwell with you — yes , take me . I am no longer worthy to look for help to the race of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: τοῖςδ ’ ὁμοῦ πέλας ,
sections.translation:
or for any good from men , creatures of a day .
No , the daughter of Zeus , the valiant goddess , abuses me to my destruction . Where , then , can a man flee ? Where can I go to find rest ?

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: φωνεῖν , ὁ πρόσθεν οὗτος οὐκ ἔτλη ποτ ’ ἄν .
sections.translation: If my past achievements go to ruin , my friends , along with such victims as these near me , and if I am inclined to foolish plunderings , then with sword driven by both hands all the army would murder me !

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: χρόνον · ἀλλ ’ οὐκ ἔτι μ ’, οὐκ
sections.translation: Ah , what misery for me that a valuable man should speak words of a sort which he would never before now have endured to speak !

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: τοῦτό τις φρονῶν ἴστω .
sections.translation: Ah ! You paths of the sounding sea , you tidal caves and wooded pastures by the shore , long , long , too long indeed

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original:
420
εὔφρονες Ἀργείοις ,
sections.translation:
have you detained me here at Troy . But no more will you hold me , no more so long as I have the breath of life . Of that much let sane men be sure .
O neighboring streams of Scamander ,

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original:
425
δέρχθη , χθονὸς μολόντ ’ ἀπὸ
sections.translation: kindly to the Greeks , no more shall you look on Ajax , whose equal in the army — here I will boast —

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original:
430
Αἰαῖ · τίς ἄν ποτ ’ ᾤεθ ’ ὧδ ’ ἐπώνυμον
sections.translation: Troy has never seen come from the land of Hellas . But now deprived of honor I lie low here in the dust !

sections.label: Section 428
sections.original: καὶ τρίς · τοιούτοις γὰρ κακοῖς ἐντυγχάνω ·
sections.translation: In truth I do not know how to restrain you , nor how to let you speak further , when you have fallen on such harsh troubles .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original:
435
τὰ πρῶτα καλλιστεῖ ’ ἀριστεύσας στρατοῦ ,
sections.translation: Aiai ! Who would ever have thought that my name would so descriptively suit my troubles ? For well now may Ajax cry Aiai — yes , twice and three times . Such are the harsh troubles with which I have met . Look , I am one whose father ’ s

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original:
440
ἄτιμος Ἀργείοισιν ὧδ ’ ἀπόλλυμαι .
sections.translation: prowess won him the fairest prize of all the army , whose father brought every glory home from this same land of Ida ; but I , his son , who came after him to this same ground of Troy with no less might and proved the service of my hand in no meaner deeds ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original:
445
νῦν δ ’ αὔτ ’ Ἀτρεῖδαι φωτὶ παντουργῷ φρένας
sections.translation: I am ruined as you see by dishonor from the Greeks . And yet of this much I feel sure : if Achilles lived , and had been called to award the first place in valor to any claimant of his arms , no one would have grasped them before me .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original:
450
νῦν δ ’ ἡ Δῖός γοργῶπις ἀδάματος θεὰ
sections.translation: But now the Atreidae have made away with them to a man without scruples and thrust away the triumphs of Ajax . And if these eyes and this warped mind had not swerved from the purpose that was mine , they would have never in this way procured votes in judgment against another man .

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: ἐμοῦ μὲν οὐχ ἑκόντος · εἰ δέ τις θεῶν 455
sections.translation: As it was , the daughter of Zeus , the grim-eyed , unconquerable goddess , tripped me up at the instant when I was readying my hand against them , and shot me with a plague of frenzy so that I might bloody my hands in these grazers . And those men exult to have escaped me —

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: πότερα πρὸς οἴκους , ναυλόχους λιπὼν ἕδρας , 460
sections.translation:
not that I wanted their escape . But if a god sends harm , it is true that even the base man can elude the worthier .
And now what shall I do , when I am plainly hated by the gods , abhorred by the Greek forces and detested by all Troy and all these plains ?
